issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for primary navigation link/button transitions
issuesFound.explanation: The `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button` selector defines a `transition` on `color` and `background-color`. No reduced‑motion override is provided, so the animation runs for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for hero image hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` selector defines a `transition` on `transform` and `box-shadow`, and the `:hover` state changes those properties. No reduced‑motion media query disables the animation, which can be problematic for users preferring reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for logo list image hover filter transition
issuesFound.explanation: `.logo-list img` defines a `transition` on `filter`. No reduced‑motion media query disables it, so the hover effect animates for users who have reduced‑motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for feature‑card hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: `.feature-card` defines a `transition` on `border-color`, `box-shadow`, and `transform`. The `:hover` state changes those properties, but there is no reduced‑motion media query to disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for product‑card hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: `.product-card` defines a `transition` on `box-shadow`, `border-color`, and `transform`. The `:hover` state changes those properties, but there is no reduced‑motion media query to disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for pricing‑card hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card` defines a `transition` on `box-shadow` and `transform`. The `:hover` state changes those properties, but no reduced‑motion override is provided.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for stat‑item hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: `.stat-item` defines a `transition` on `background-color` and `transform`. The `:hover` state changes those properties, but there is no reduced‑motion media query to disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for testimonial‑card hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: `.testimonial-card` defines a `transition` on `box-shadow` and `border-color`. The `:hover` state changes those properties, but no reduced‑motion media query disables the animation.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for FAQ item transition
issuesFound.explanation: `.faq-item` defines a `transition` on `border-color` and `box-shadow`. The `:focus-visible` and `:hover` states modify those properties, but there is no reduced‑motion media query to disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for social‑link hover transition
issuesFound.explanation: `.social-links a` defines a `transition` on `color`. The hover state changes the color, but no reduced‑motion media query disables the transition.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for social‑link image hover transition
issuesFound.explanation: `.social-links img` defines a `transition` on `opacity`. The hover state changes opacity, but no reduced‑motion media query disables the transition.

issuesFound.title: Custom billing‑toggle element missing keyboard activation (Enter/Space) for click handler
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `data-billing-toggle` is given a click listener (`_toggleEl.addEventListener('click', _onToggleClick)`) but only an Escape key handler is attached. When the element is not a native `<button>` or `<a>`, it lacks keyboard support for activating the toggle via Enter or Space, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

issuesFound.title: CSS removes focus indicators without providing visible :focus-visible alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The CSS rule `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }` removes focus indicators for mouse users but does not ensure a visible focus style is consistently applied via `:focus-visible` for all interactive elements. Keyboard users may lose focus visibility, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion guard for CSS animations/transitions
issuesFound.explanation: CSS animations and transitions (e.g., `transition`, `transform`, `hover` effects) are defined without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` guard. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience vestibular or distraction issues, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Base font size below 16px (12px) on html element
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` element sets `font-size: 12px`, which is below the recommended minimum of 16px for readability. Users with low vision or cognitive disabilities may struggle to read text, violating WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text (Level AA) and 1.4.8 Visual Presentation (Level AAA).

issuesFound.title: Base font size below recommended 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which sets the rem unit to 12px. This results in body text of 12px (1rem), which is below the 16px recommended for readable text and may cause readability issues for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 148:   font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line height below recommended 1.5
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph elements have line-height: 1, which is below the WCAG recommended 1.5 for body text. This tight spacing reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 196:   line-height: var(--line-height-normal);

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only class fails to visually hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which keeps the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. This breaks the intended behavior for screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Reduced motion media query does not disable animations
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, so it does not suppress transitions and animations for users who prefer reduced motion. This can cause vestibular disorders or discomfort for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus trap not activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap() or activates it when the menu opens. When the mobile menu opens, keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer, and focus is not returned to the trigger button when the menu closes, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2170: Initialize the trap in init(): `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true });` and activate it in open(): `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: Contact form missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for full name (contact-first-name/contact-last-name) and email (contact-email) collect personal user data but lack autocomplete attributes. This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from auto-filling known values, violating WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4260: Add autocomplete attribute in the field configuration or binding: For contactFirstName use `autocomplete: 'given-name'`, for contactLastName use `autocomplete: 'family-name'`, and for contactEmail use `autocomplete: 'email'`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input collects personal data (email address) but lacks an autocomplete attribute, preventing automatic filling by user agents and assistive technologies, violating WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4140: After selecting the email input, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form fields for name (wizard_name) and email (wizard_email) contain personal data signals in their name attributes but lack autocomplete attributes (e.g., "name", "email"), violating WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4438: Add autocomplete attributes to the field configurations: for wizard_name use `autocomplete: 'name'` and for wizard_email use `autocomplete: 'email'`.

issuesFound.title: Toast notification auto-dismiss timer lacks pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toast notification uses setTimeout to auto-dismiss after a duration (dur) without providing a mechanism to pause, extend, or stop the timer on hover or focus. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable) as users with reading disabilities may not have sufficient time to read the notification before it disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2132: Replace the simple setTimeout with a pausable timer implementation that listens for mouseenter/mouseleave and focusin/focusout events on the toast element to pause and resume the countdown.

issuesFound.title: Toast notification conflicting ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The toast element is created with role="alert" (which implicitly has aria-live="assertive") but also explicitly sets aria-live="polite" for non-error types. This creates conflicting accessibility semantics and may cause unpredictable screen reader announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2108: Remove the [ARIA.LIVE] property from the createElement call. For error toasts, rely on role="alert" alone; for other types, use role="status" instead of role="alert" to imply polite announcements.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation error message conflicting ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The inline error message span is created with both role="alert" and aria-live="polite". Since role="alert" implies aria-live="assertive", these attributes conflict, potentially causing inconsistent screen reader behavior when field validation errors occur.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3572: Change role: 'alert' to role: 'status' for inline validation messages, or remove the aria-live property and rely solely on the implicit behavior of the role.

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar missing required role
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard updates aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext on the _progressBar element, but the code never ensures this element has role="progressbar". Without this role, the ARIA value attributes are invalid and may not be conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4320: After `_progressBar = qs('[data-wizard-progress]', _form);`, add `setAttr(_progressBar, 'role', 'progressbar');` and also set aria-valuemin="0" and aria-valuemax="100" if not present in the HTML.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked state
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing toggle is not a native button, the code assigns role="switch" but never updates the aria-checked attribute to reflect the current state (annual vs monthly). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the state of the switch is not programmatically determined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3956: After setting the role, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`. Then in the setPeriod function (around line 3985), add `if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(period === 'year'));` to keep the state synchronized.

issuesFound.title: Automatic alt text fallback hides informative images
issuesFound.explanation: The auditImages function automatically sets alt="" on any image lacking an alt attribute, treating all such images as decorative. If the page contains informative images without alt text (e.g., in user-generated content or missed by developers), this script hides them from screen readers, violating WCAG 1.1.1 (Non-text Content).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3246: Remove the line `img.setAttribute('alt', '');` from the auditImages function. The function should only warn about missing alt text in the console without modifying the DOM, leaving the images accessible to AT as having missing alt (which some screen readers will announce as "image" rather than ignoring them entirely).

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard step panels have role="tabpanel" without required tablist parent or tab roles on navigation buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The code assigns role="tabpanel" to each wizard step panel, but the navigation buttons (prev/next) are not contained in a role="tablist" and do not have role="tab". This breaks the ARIA tab pattern and causes assistive technologies to not recognize the relationship between the navigation and the content panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3654-3658: Remove role="tabpanel" from step panels and use a standard wizard pattern with aria-live regions for step announcements, or add role="tablist" to the navigation container and role="tab" to the prev/next buttons with appropriate aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: Product filter view mode toggles lack keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The delegated click handler for '[data-view-mode]' elements does not include keyboard support (Enter/Space). If the toggle elements are not native <button> elements, keyboard users cannot activate them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2882: Add keyboard event delegation for Enter and Space keys, or ensure the selector only targets <button> elements by adding role="button" and tabindex="0" to non-button elements before binding events.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger may not be keyboard focusable or have correct role
issuesFound.explanation: The register function adds a keydown listener to the trigger element but does not ensure the element is focusable (tabindex="0") or has role="button" if it is not a native button. Keyboard users cannot reach or activate the trigger if it is a generic element.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks Enter/Space keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: The _onTriggerKeydown handler only responds to Escape key. If the trigger element is not a native button, keyboard users cannot activate it with Enter or Space to open the navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2143-2148: Add cases for KEYS.ENTER and KEYS.SPACE that call the toggle() function:
  Line 2144: case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); toggle(); break;

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer opens without moving focus or trapping focus

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation missing focus trap and focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer does not create or activate a focus trap when opened, and does not return focus to the trigger button when closed. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible). Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the page content behind it, and when the drawer closes, focus is lost rather than returned to the hamburger button.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion aria-expanded not set when opening

The `close` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, but the `open` function omits setting it to "true". This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) — screen readers cannot accurately announce the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu aria-expanded not managed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller does not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button when opening or closing dropdowns. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) — screen readers cannot announce whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked state
